source_paragraph: company goals by proposing a novel recommendation system for internal business processes, grounded in the COBIT 2019 framework. The system employs graph algorithms to map relationships, semantic similarity measure- ments to align enterprise goals with management objectives, and LSTM combined with TF-IDF methods to analyze and recommend specific activities. Experimental results show that this system optimizes IT management practices, enhances operational efficiency, and ensures regulatory compliance. Additionally, a user-friendly graphical representation of internal business pro- cesses using Neo4j enables stakeholders to visualize complex relationships within the COBIT framework, facilitating informed decision-making. This research advances IT governance practices by providing organizations with a valuable tool to automate and improve IT strategies, effectively achieving business objectives. In the context of COBIT 2019, Neo4j can be utilized to build dynamic graphical representations of various COBIT elements . By using Neo4j, organizations can create dynamic and interactive graphical models to better understand and manage the complex relationships between different COBIT 2019 elements . The contribution of this paper is in the receiver processing of CC-OFDM-MIMO radar, which is different from those in other types of OFDM MIMO radars or other multi-TRM radars due to the nature of the orthogonal multi-beam generation with Golay-coded OFDM waveforms and subbands. There are previous research on radar waveform for ISAC purposes , which discussed about cyclic prefix (CP) based OFDM waveform and how the CP is very much defining the maximum unambiguous range of the radar system. The simulation results show that the coupling between beams and symbols are very low as the result of codes and sub-band scheduling at the transmitter. These results are valid for the three scenarios whether the targets are in the same beam, at the nearest neighboring beams and at the nearest co-channel beams. It shows that with the concept proposed in CC-OFDM MIMO Radar, it is possible to design a long-range radar with low power transmission by transmitting multi-orthogonal CC-OFDM symbols in series, to improve the Signal-to-Noise of the radar system.

source_paragraph: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) , is a variation of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) that has been successfully applied to various Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks. LSTM has the ability to capture long-range dependencies with sequences making it suitable for solving sequence labeling problems , , , such as Named Entity Recognition tagging (POS-Tag) and Neural (NER), Part-of-Speech combining (NMT). Study Machine Translation Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) model with Conditional Random Field (CRF) to solve NER and POS-Tag Task. BiLSTM encodes word or character vectors into features, while CRF is a classifier to predict the target labels. However, detecting abnormalities in log data can be performed using a point-based approach, where one single log record is the only feature needed. However, in a digital forensic setting, where the detection is performed on all collected artefacts which might be large in size, the number of the abnormalities is likely to be large . Thus, analysing the detection result in a binary setting is impractical for the investigator. Moreover, out of all detected peculiarities, there might be several negligible ones with less severe impacts on the system or less likely to be related to an incident. source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

retrieved_chunk: This paper examines the parameter identifiability, i.e., the maximum number of detectable targets uniquely identified and the detection performance, i.e., the probabilities of detection and false alarm, of the Full PMIMO radar (FPMIMO), which is a further generalization of combination of the PA and MIMO concepts that uses overlapping subarrays on both transmit and receive arrays.

source_paragraph: a speciﬁed time delay difference of (cid:49)t, each (cid:49)t value produces a beam pattern equivalent to a phased array having inter-element phase difference that depends on the corresponding subcarrier frequencies. Multiple beams can be formed simultaneously, by assigning multiple subcarrier sets which are transmit- ted using different value of (cid:49)t. This idea is the primary contribution of this paper, which will be elaborated in the sequel. A second contribution in this paper concerns the improvement of time on target in a MIMO radar adopting CC-OFDM waveforms. REQUIREMENTS To elucidate the novel concept and detailed implementation of the CC-OFDM MIMO radar, we will take a surveillance radar design as an example for analyses in the subsequent sections. The CC-OFDM MIMO radar waveform designed herein is based on the requirement of surveillance radars used in coastal areas, where the target of interest are ships and small aircrafts in the area within 180 km from the radar site. OFDM signals with subcarrier spacing of 10 KHz will give a maximum unambiguous range of 15 km. source_paragraph: I. INTRODUCTION I. INTRODUCTION The use of drones has become increasingly common in various applications, including aerial photography, surveying, and monitoring. However, drones can also be involved in incidents such as accidents, violations, or even crimes, making it necessary to investigate drone logs as part of forensic investigations. In a forensic investigation, drone logs are essential sources of information that can help investigators reconstruct the events leading up to an incident. Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Important analysis of drone logs involves the identification of the most critical pieces of information in the log data and the analysis of how they relate to the incident

source_paragraph: presented a novel multi-head attention mechanism that uses various atten- tion functions to capture diverse contextual elements. The multi-head attention approach allows attention to be simul- taneously allocated to information from different subspaces and representational positions. In contrast to soft attention, which focuses attention softly on the entire input to produce a context representation with weights determined based on the relevance of each part of the input, soft attention relies on only one attention mechanism. Kumar et al. developed the multi-head attention- based BiLSTM (MHA-BiLSTM) model. The results are quite promising compared to the use of single-head attention. The choice between one-stage and two-stage object detec- tion models depends heavily on the specific demands of the task, particularly in medical imaging contexts such as colorectal cancer (CRC), squamous cell carcinoma (SCC), gastric lesions, and polypoid lesions. One-stage detectors, such as YOLO and SSD, excel in speed and computational efficiency, making them ideal for real-time applications. For instance, YOLOv5 demonstrated remarkable performance in CRC detection with nearly perfect metrics—precision (99.071%), recall (98.064%), and mAP (98.8%)—allowing clinicians to analyze colonoscopy videos efficiently during live procedures . source_paragraph: DATASET The DEAP dataset has 32 participants with a composition of 50% male and 50% female samples . The stimulus used is a video with a duration of 60 seconds. The results of the EEG recording have a duration of 63 seconds, of which 3 seconds are the baseline. One participant has 40 recordings of the EEG trials. The results of the EEG trial recordings have been mea- sured using the Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM). SAM has four emotional dimensions, namely valence, arousal, domi- nance, and liking, with ratings 0-9. For valence and arousal classes, emotion labels are divided into two categories: low and high.
